message:
feat(quality): Implement pre-release quality gates (bd-1jt.3.11)

- Add src/quality_gates.sh module with:
  - qg_get_checks() to read checks from repos.yaml
  - qg_run_checks() to execute quality checks with timing
  - Support for --dry-run and --skip-checks modes
  - Per-check exit code and duration tracking
  - JSON output with passed/failed counts

- Add 'dsr quality' command:
  - --tool <name> to specify which tool's checks to run
  - --work-dir to specify execution directory
  - JSON envelope output in --json mode
  - Exit 1 when any check fails

Closed: bd-1jt.3.11

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.11","title":"Implement build workspace isolation + lock/state machine","description":"# Implement Build Workspace Isolation + Lock/State Machine

## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.12","title":"Define artifact naming + build manifest schema","description":"# Define Artifact Naming + Build Manifest Schema

## Purpose
Standardize artifact names and produce a manifest JSON that release/install/tests can trust.

## Naming Contract
- `{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]`
- `checksums.sha256` includes all binaries
- Optional `.minisig` for each artifact

## Manifest Schema (minimum)
- `tool`, `version`, `run_id`, `git_sha`, `schema_version`
- `artifacts[]`: name, target, sha256, size_bytes, signature_path
- `hosts[]`: host, status, duration_ms, log_path, retry_count
- `build_env`: toolchain versions, workflow/job metadata, act version

## Placement
- `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>.json`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest produced for every build
- [ ] Release + installer use manifest as source of truth
- [ ] Tests validate manifest schema
- [ ] Manifest includes toolchain + host metadata for auditability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:54:50.125203319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:35.088175561Z","close_reason":"Created schemas/manifest.json (JSON Schema for build manifests), docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md (comprehensive naming convention docs), and scripts/tests/fixtures/manifest-example.json (test fixture). Defines {tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch} naming, manifest structure with artifacts, hosts, signatures, and SBOM references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.13","title":"Create act workflow compatibility matrix + overrides","description":"# Create act Workflow Compatibility Matrix + Overrides

## Purpose
Ensure each tool's release workflow can be executed locally via act with correct job names, runner images, and artifact paths.

## Deliverables
- Matrix: tool → workflow file → job names → act flags
- Known exceptions (jobs not runnable in act)
- Per-host overrides (linux/amd64 vs linux/arm64)
- `repos.d/<tool>.yaml` `act_job_map` entries for each tool

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each tool has an act-compatible mapping
- [ ] Unsupported jobs documented and skipped
- [ ] Artifact paths consistent across tools
- [ ] Overrides documented for non-standard workflows","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:52.346436490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:13:37.582165657Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:13:37.582147743Z","close_reason":"Created ACT_COMPATIBILITY.md matrix document and repos.d/ configuration templates (_template_rust.yaml, _template_go.yaml, ntm.yaml.example). Updated .gitignore for repos.d files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:52.346436490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:31.371618554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.14","title":"Build from tag/commit for reproducibility","description":"# Build From Tag/Commit for Reproducibility

## Purpose
Ensure fallback builds are reproducible by building from the exact tag/commit that will be released.

## Behavior
- Resolve version → tag → commit SHA
- Support explicit `--ref <sha|tag>` override
- Use fresh workspace (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
- Record git SHA in manifest + logs
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations

## Git Plumbing (No Status Parsing)
- Dirty check: `git diff-index --quiet HEAD --` (exit code)
- Tag existence: `git show-ref --tags --verify refs/tags/<tag>`
- Resolve SHA: `git rev-parse <ref>`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds use tagged commit, not local HEAD
- [ ] Dirty working trees produce clear errors
- [ ] Manifest contains git SHA + resolved ref
- [ ] `--ref` supports both tags and full SHAs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:10.707996895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:35:52.120988167Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:52.253058133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:54.701273926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:10.707996895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:56.742094781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:59.320434061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1jt.2.14","title":"Build from tag/commit for reproducibility","description":"# Build From Tag/Commit for Reproducibility

## Purpose
Ensure fallback builds are reproducible by building from the exact tag/commit that will be released.

## Behavior
- Resolve version → tag → commit SHA
- Support explicit `--ref <sha|tag>` override
- Use fresh workspace (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)

- Dirty check: `git diff-index --quiet HEAD --` (exit code)

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.15","title":"Host selection + concurrency limits per host","description":"# Host Selection + Concurrency Limits Per Host

## Purpose
Choose the best host for each target based on health and enforce per-host concurrency to avoid overload.

## Behavior
- Select host using health + capability matrix from `hosts.yaml`
- Allow overrides: `--host`, `--max-parallel`, per-host concurrency caps
- Queue builds when a host is at capacity (FIFO with run_id ordering)
- Emit JSON details for scheduling decisions

## Design Notes
- Deterministic selection: same inputs → same host choice
- Never exceed per-host concurrency limits
- Record queue decisions in state/logs for postmortems

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host selection deterministic and configurable
- [ ] Per-host concurrency limits enforced
- [ ] Queue/defer decisions logged with run_id + target
- [ ] JSON output includes host selection rationale","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:17.923979053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:53:04.514928295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:08.198973905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:17.923979053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:15.521885678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:13.167796475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.16","title":"Artifact retention + prune command (disk pressure guard)","description":"# Artifact Retention + Prune Command (Disk Pressure Guard)

## Purpose
Prevent disk exhaustion by pruning old build artifacts and cached downloads safely.

## Behavior
- `dsr prune --dry-run` shows what would be removed
- Retention policy configurable (keep last N runs per tool)
- Require explicit confirmation unless `--yes`
- Log every pruned path to state logs
- Hard safety: refuse to delete outside `DSR_STATE_DIR` and `DSR_CACHE_DIR`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safe dry-run output
- [ ] Retention policy configurable
- [ ] Prune logs record what was removed
- [ ] Guardrails prevent accidental deletion of non-dsr paths","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:10:13.480918878Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:46.986203142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:23.490018340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:26.407796120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:41.648734026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:46.778018160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1jt.2.16","title":"Artifact retention + prune command (disk pressure guard)","description":"# Artifact Retention + Prune Command (Disk Pressure Guard)

## Purpose
Prevent disk exhaustion by pruning old build artifacts and cached downloads safely.

## Behavior
- `dsr prune --dry-run` shows what would be removed
- Retention policy configurable (keep last N runs per tool)
- Require explicit confirmation unless `--yes`
- Log every pruned path to state logs
- Hard safety: refuse to delete outside `DSR_STATE_DIR` and `DSR_CACHE_DIR`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safe dry-run output
- [ ] Retention policy configurable
- [ ] Prune logs record what was removed
- [ ] Guardrails prevent accidental deletion of non-dsr paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:58.834957229Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:58.834930970Z","close_reason":"Implemented prune command","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:46.986203142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:23.490018340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:26.407796120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:41.648734026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:46.778018160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.2","title":"Setup Linux build environment on Threadripper (trj)","description":"# Setup Linux Build Environment on Threadripper (trj)

## Purpose
Ensure the local Linux host (trj) has all toolchains needed to build any tool.

## Host Details
- Alias: trj
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux (x86_64)

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Rust Nightly
```bash
# Install rustup if missing
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly toolchain
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Install cross-compilation targets
rustup target add x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
rustup target add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
rustup target add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl

# Install cargo-zigbuild for easy cross-compilation
cargo install cargo-zigbuild
```

### 2. Go (Latest)
```bash
# Download and install
wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.23.linux-amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.23.linux-amd64.tar.gz
echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' >> ~/.bashrc

# Verify
go version
```

### 3. Bun (Latest)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
```

### 4. Zig (for cargo-zigbuild)
```bash
# cargo-zigbuild needs zig installed
snap install zig --classic --beta
# or
wget https://ziglang.org/download/0.13.0/zig-linux-x86_64-0.13.0.tar.xz
tar xf zig-linux-x86_64-0.13.0.tar.xz
sudo mv zig-linux-x86_64-0.13.0 /usr/local/zig
echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/zig' >> ~/.bashrc
```

### 5. Build Dependencies
```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y   build-essential   pkg-config   libssl-dev   mingw-w64 \           # For Windows cross-compilation
  gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu  # For ARM64 cross-compilation
```

## Verification Script
Use `scripts/toolchain_check.sh` (repo) or create a local verifier:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification ==="
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Zig: "; zig version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "MinGW: "; x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "ARM64 GCC: "; aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Done ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] cargo-zigbuild can cross-compile to Windows
- [ ] cargo-zigbuild can cross-compile to ARM64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile to all targets
- [ ] Bun can compile standalone binaries
- [ ] Verification script exits 0

## Maintenance
- Add a weekly toolchain check (cron or manual)
- Document upgrade procedures for each toolchain","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:52.965747343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:21:19.693805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:52.965747343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.3","title":"Setup macOS build environment on Mac mini (mmini)","description":"# Setup macOS Build Environment on Mac mini

## Purpose
Ensure the Mac mini (mmini) has all toolchains needed to build macOS binaries.

## Host Details
- Alias: mmini
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon ARM64)
- User: jemanuel
- Sudo: Passwordless via /etc/sudoers.d/jemanuel

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Homebrew (Package Manager)
\`\`\`bash
# Should already be installed, verify:
brew --version

# If missing:
/bin/bash -c "\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
\`\`\`

### 2. Rust Nightly
\`\`\`bash
# Install rustup
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Add x86_64 target for universal builds
rustup target add x86_64-apple-darwin
\`\`\`

### 3. Go (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install go
# or download from go.dev for specific version
\`\`\`

### 4. Bun (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install oven-sh/bun/bun
# or
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
\`\`\`

### 5. Xcode Command Line Tools
\`\`\`bash
xcode-select --install
# Needed for: clang, ar, ranlib, etc.
\`\`\`

## Universal Binary Support
macOS can build both ARM64 and x86_64:

### Rust Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build for both architectures
cargo build --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin
cargo build --release --target x86_64-apple-darwin

# Create universal binary with lipo
lipo -create \\
  target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

### Go Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build ARM64
GOARCH=arm64 go build -o mytool-arm64 ./cmd/mytool

# Build x86_64
GOARCH=amd64 go build -o mytool-amd64 ./cmd/mytool

# Create universal
lipo -create mytool-arm64 mytool-amd64 -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

## Verification Script
Create ~/bin/dsr-verify-toolchains.sh:
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (macOS) ==="
echo -n "Homebrew: "; brew --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Xcode CLT: "; xcode-select -p || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "lipo: "; which lipo || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Rust Targets ==="
rustup target list --installed
echo "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] Rust can build for both ARM64 and x86_64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile for both architectures
- [ ] lipo available for creating universal binaries
- [ ] Verification script exits 0

## Notes
- Mac mini is accessed via Tailscale, ensure daemon is running
- Apple Silicon can run x86_64 binaries via Rosetta 2
- Consider code signing for distribution (future enhancement)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:29.631435925Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.4","title":"CRITICAL: Use nektos/act to run GH Actions YAML locally","description":"# Use nektos/act to Run GH Actions Workflows Locally

## Purpose
Reuse existing `.github/workflows/*.yml` to build releases locally when GH Actions is throttled.

## Key `act` Capabilities (from official docs)
- Run a specific workflow: `act -W .github/workflows/release.yml`
- Run a specific job: `act -j build-linux`
- List jobs: `act -l`
- Override runner images: `-P ubuntu-latest=...`
- Set container architecture: `--container-architecture linux/arm64`
- Control pulls / offline mode: `--pull=false` / `--action-offline-mode`
- Route artifacts: `--artifact-server-path /tmp/act-artifacts`

## Critical Limitations
- `act` runs workflows in **Docker** (Linux). It **cannot** execute macOS/Windows runner jobs.
- macOS/Windows builds must be native (mmini, wlap) or cross-compiled.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `act` runs `release.yml` for Linux targets
- [ ] Runner images pinned via `~/.actrc`
- [ ] Artifact collection path standardized
- [ ] Offline mode works once images are cached

## References
- https://github.com/nektos/act

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3","title":"Theme 3: Release Upload Automation","description":"# Release Upload Automation

## Purpose
After local builds complete, create GitHub releases and upload binaries with proper checksums and signatures.

## The dsr release Command
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>           # Create release, upload all builds
dsr release <tool> <version> --draft   # Create as draft first
dsr release <tool> <version> --notes "..." # Custom release notes
```

## Release Workflow

### 1. Verify Builds Exist
```bash
# Check all expected artifacts exist
build_dir="$DSR_STATE_DIR/builds/$tool/$version"
for target in linux-amd64 linux-arm64 darwin-arm64 darwin-amd64 windows-amd64; do
  artifact="$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-${target}"
  [ -f "$artifact" ] || die "Missing: $artifact"
done
```

### 2. Generate Checksums (No global cd)
```bash
# Generate checksums in place
sha256sum "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-* > "$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-checksums.sha256"
```

### 3. Sign Artifacts (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
# Using minisign
for f in "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*; do
  minisign -Sm "$f" -s ~/.minisign/key.sec
done
```

### 4. Create GitHub Release
```bash
gh release create "$version" \
  --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" \
  --title "$tool $version" \
  --notes "$release_notes" \
  "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*
```

### 5. Verify Upload
```bash
gh release view "$version" --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" --json assets
```

## Release Notes Generation (Git Plumbing)
```bash
repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
last_tag=$(git -C "$repo_dir" describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^)
git -C "$repo_dir" log --oneline ${last_tag}..HEAD > "$build_dir/release_notes.txt"
```

## Asset Naming Convention
```
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]
{tool}-{version}-checksums.sha256
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}.minisig
```

Examples:
```
ntm-v1.5.1-linux-amd64
ntm-v1.5.1-darwin-arm64
ntm-v1.5.1-windows-amd64.exe
ntm-v1.5.1-checksums.sha256
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates release with correct tag
- [ ] All binaries uploaded and downloadable
- [ ] Checksums file included
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated if not provided
- [ ] Works for all 16 tools

## Error Handling
- Release already exists → Prompt to update or abort
- Upload fails → Retry, then report which assets failed
- Tag doesn't exist → Create tag or error","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:45.249464757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.1","title":"Implement dsr release command - upload artifacts to GitHub","description":"# Implement `dsr release` — Upload Artifacts to GitHub

## Purpose
Create a GitHub Release from locally built artifacts, with retries, verification, and consistent metadata.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --artifacts <dir>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --draft
 dsr release <tool> <version> --generate-notes
 dsr release <tool> <version> --verify-tag
 dsr release <tool> <version> --dry-run
 dsr release <tool> <version> --resume
```

## Required Inputs
- Build manifest (source of truth)
- Checksums + signatures
- Optional SBOM + provenance

## Release Flow
1. Validate manifest + checksums
2. Create release tag (verify exists)
3. Upload binaries + checksums + signatures
4. Upload SBOM + provenance if configured
5. Verify assets match manifest

## Idempotency / Resume
- Safe to re-run (`gh release upload --clobber`)
- Track partial uploads in state for resume

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release created with correct tag & notes
- [ ] All assets uploaded and verified
- [ ] Works for all tools
- [ ] JSON report includes asset list + validation results
- [ ] Resume re-uploads only missing/failed assets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:53.575381627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:50.900084392Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:50.900049447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.228966845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:41.414912067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.386430911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:53.575381627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:52.882491227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:54.944683486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.751889231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:52:05.559597020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.10","title":"Implement release verification + retry/resume uploads","description":"# Implement Release Verification + Retry/Resume Uploads

## Purpose
Ensure GitHub releases contain the exact expected assets, and recover safely from partial upload failures.

## Verification Steps
- Compare release assets against build manifest
- Verify checksums.sha256 matches uploaded binaries
- Optionally re-download and verify sha256 for spot checks

## Retry Strategy
- Use `gh release upload --clobber` for failed assets
- Track failed uploads in state file for resume
- Idempotent uploads (safe to re-run)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing assets detected and reported
- [ ] Partial uploads can be resumed safely
- [ ] Release verification produces JSON report
- [ ] Re-running verification does not create duplicate assets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:55:33.160035551Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:12.415821448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.651659081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:40.580783022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Purpose
Ensure every release passes the tool's own quality checks before upload to GitHub.

## Behavior
- Read `checks` from `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Run checks in deterministic order
- Support `--skip-checks` and `--dry-run`
- Log per-check duration + exit code
- Fail release if any check fails (exit 1/6 depending on stage)

## Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout (JSON): list of checks, durations, exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checks run per-tool before release upload
- [ ] Failures block release with clear errors
- [ ] JSON output includes per-check results
- [ ] Dry-run shows planned checks","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:25:01.547187732Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:21.256627491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:47.402939311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.2","title":"Update Homebrew and Scoop formulas after release","description":"# Update Homebrew and Scoop Formulas After Release

## Purpose
Update package manager metadata so users can install via `brew` and `scoop` with correct URLs + SHA256.

## Homebrew
- Update formula in `homebrew-tap`
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp` (never `/data/projects`)
- Validate with `brew audit --strict` and `brew style` when available
- Respect `--dry-run` (plan only)

## Scoop
- Update manifest JSON in `scoop-bucket`
- Use `sha256:<hex>` format
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp`
- Validate JSON and run `scoop checkver` when available

## Logging / Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout: JSON (when `--json`) listing updated formula/manifest paths + checksums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula/manifest updated with correct version + hashes
- [ ] Dry-run available and deterministic
- [ ] Validation commands executed when available
- [ ] Works for all tools without touching `/data/projects`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629580772Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:56.108363071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629546588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.3","title":"Implement minisign artifact signing","description":"# Implement Minisign Artifact Signing

## Purpose
Sign every release artifact so users can verify authenticity.

## Requirements
- Sign every binary + checksums.sha256
- Store signatures as `.minisig` files
- Use password-protected key from secrets store

## Installer Integration
- Verify signatures when minisign present
- Fail when `--require-signatures` is set

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All binaries + checksum file signed
- [ ] Signatures uploaded with releases
- [ ] Installers verify signatures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:59.730105373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:16:01.600966648Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:16:01.600949125Z","close_reason":"Minisign signing module complete in src/signing.sh: keypair generation (init), configuration check, single file signing (sign), batch signing (sign_batch), signature verification (verify), public key extraction (pubkey), permission fixing (fix). +# Quality gates command - run pre-release checks
+cmd_quality() {
+    local tool_name=""
+    local dry_run=false
+    local skip_checks=false
+    local work_dir=""
+
+    # Parse arguments
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --tool|-t)
+                tool_name="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --dry-run|-n)
+                dry_run=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --skip-checks)
+                skip_checks=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --work-dir|-w)
+                work_dir="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --help|-h)
+                cat << 'EOF'
+dsr quality - Run pre-release quality checks
+
+USAGE:
+    dsr quality --tool <name> [options]
+
+OPTIONS:
+    -t, --tool <name>    Tool to check (required)
+    -n, --dry-run        Show checks without running them
+    --skip-checks        Skip all checks (for testing)
+    -w, --work-dir       Directory to run checks in
+
+DESCRIPTION:
+    Runs quality gate checks configured in repos.yaml for the specified tool.
+    Checks are commands like 'cargo test', 'go vet', etc.
+
+CONFIGURATION:
+    Configure checks in ~/.config/dsr/repos.yaml:
+
+    tools:
+      ntm:
+        checks:
+          - "cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings"
+          - "cargo test"
+          - "cargo fmt --check"
+
+EXAMPLES:
+    dsr quality --tool ntm             Run checks for ntm
+    dsr quality --tool ntm --dry-run   Show what would run
+    dsr quality --tool bv -w /path/to/bv
+
+EXIT CODES:
+    0  - All checks passed
+    1  - One or more checks failed
+    4  - Invalid arguments
+EOF
+                return 0
+                ;;
+            *)
+                if [[ -z "$tool_name" ]]; then
+                    tool_name="$1"
+                else
+                    log_error "Unknown argument: $1"
+                    return 4
+                fi
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    if [[ -z "$tool_name" ]]; then
+        log_error "Tool name required. Use --tool <name> or provide as first argument."
+        log_info "Run 'dsr quality --help' for usage"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    # Build qg_run_checks arguments
+    local qg_args=("$tool_name")
+    $dry_run && qg_args+=("--dry-run")
+    $skip_checks && qg_args+=("--skip-checks")
+    [[ -n "$work_dir" ]] && qg_args+=("--work-dir" "$work_dir")
+
+    # Run quality checks
+    local result exit_code=0
+    result=$(qg_run_checks "${qg_args[@]}") || exit_code=$?
+
+    # Output
+    if $JSON_MODE; then
+        json_envelope "quality" "$([ $exit_code -eq 0 ] && echo success || echo failure)" "$exit_code" "$result"
+    fi
+
+    return $exit_code
+}
+
 cmd_release() {
     log_warn "release command not yet implemented"
     log_info "See bead bd-1jt.3.1 for implementation plan"
@@ -2788,6 +2891,9 @@ main() {
         fallback)
             cmd_fallback "$@"
             ;;
+        quality)
+            cmd_quality "$@"
+            ;;
         repos)
             cmd_repos "$@"
             ;;

@@ -0,0 +1,310 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# quality_gates.sh - Pre-release quality gates for dsr
+#
+# Runs configured quality checks (lint, test, typecheck) before release.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   source quality_gates.sh
+#   qg_run_checks <tool_name> [--dry-run] [--skip-checks]
+#   qg_get_checks <tool_name>  # list configured checks
+#
+# Check Configuration (in repos.yaml):
+#   tools:
+#     ntm:
+#       checks:
+#         - "cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings"
+#         - "cargo test"
+#         - "cargo fmt --check"
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+# Colors for output
+if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && -t 2 ]]; then
+    _QG_GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
+    _QG_RED=$'\033[0;31m'
+    _QG_YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
+    _QG_BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
+    _QG_GRAY=$'\033[0;90m'
+    _QG_NC=$'\033[0m'
+else
+    _QG_GREEN='' _QG_RED='' _QG_YELLOW='' _QG_BLUE='' _QG_GRAY='' _QG_NC=''
+fi
+
+_qg_log_info()  { echo "${_QG_BLUE}[quality]${_QG_NC} $*" >&2; }
+_qg_log_ok()    { echo "${_QG_GREEN}[quality]${_QG_NC} $*" >&2; }
+_qg_log_warn()  { echo "${_QG_YELLOW}[quality]${_QG_NC} $*" >&2; }
+_qg_log_error() { echo "${_QG_RED}[quality]${_QG_NC} $*" >&2; }
+
+# Get configured checks for a tool
+# Usage: qg_get_checks <tool_name>
+# Returns: JSON array of check commands
+qg_get_checks() {
+    local tool_name="$1"
+
+    if ! command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
+        _qg_log_error "yq required for reading tool configuration"
+        echo '[]'
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    local repos_file="${DSR_REPOS_FILE:-${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}/repos.yaml}"
+    if [[ ! -f "$repos_file" ]]; then
+        _qg_log_warn "Repos file not found: $repos_file"
+        echo '[]'
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local checks
+    checks=$(yq -o=json ".tools.${tool_name}.checks // []" "$repos_file" 2>/dev/null) || checks='[]'
+    echo "$checks"
+}
+
+# Run a single check command
+# Usage: _qg_run_single_check <command> <work_dir> <dry_run>
+# Returns: JSON object with result
+_qg_run_single_check() {
+    local cmd="$1"
+    local work_dir="$2"
+    local dry_run="$3"
+
+    local start_ms end_ms duration_ms exit_code=0 output=""
+
+    # Get start time
+    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
+        start_ms=$(python3 -c 'import time; print(int(time.time() * 1000))')
+    else
+        start_ms=$(($(date +%s) * 1000))
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
+        _qg_log_info "(dry-run) Would run: $cmd"
+        output="dry-run: skipped"
+        exit_code=0
+    else
+        _qg_log_info "Running: $cmd"
+
+        # Run the command in the work directory
+        if [[ -n "$work_dir" && -d "$work_dir" ]]; then
+            output=$(cd "$work_dir" && eval "$cmd" 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
+        else
+            output=$(eval "$cmd" 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Get end time
+    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
+        end_ms=$(python3 -c 'import time; print(int(time.time() * 1000))')
+    else
+        end_ms=$(($(date +%s) * 1000))
+    fi
+
+    duration_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
+
+    # Log result
+    if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
+        _qg_log_ok "  ✓ Passed (${duration_ms}ms)"
+    else
+        _qg_log_error "  ✗ Failed (exit code: $exit_code)"
+    fi
+
+    # Escape output for JSON
+    local escaped_output
+    escaped_output=$(echo "$output" | head -c 1000 | jq -Rs '.')
+
+    # Return JSON result
+    jq -nc \
+        --arg cmd "$cmd" \
+        --argjson exit_code "$exit_code" \
+        --argjson duration_ms "$duration_ms" \
+        --argjson output "$escaped_output" \
+        '{
+            command: $cmd,
+            exit_code: $exit_code,
+            duration_ms: $duration_ms,
+            passed: ($exit_code == 0),
+            output: $output
+        }'
+}
+
+# Run all quality checks for a tool
+# Usage: qg_run_checks <tool_name> [options]
+# Options:
+#   --dry-run       Show checks without running them
+#   --skip-checks   Skip all checks (return success)
+#   --work-dir      Directory to run checks in
+# Returns: JSON object with results, exits with appropriate code
+qg_run_checks() {
+    local tool_name=""
+    local dry_run=false
+    local skip_checks=false
+    local work_dir=""
+
+    # Parse arguments
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --dry-run|-n)
+                dry_run=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --skip-checks)
+                skip_checks=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --work-dir)
+                work_dir="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --help|-h)
+                cat << 'EOF'
+Usage: qg_run_checks <tool_name> [options]
+
+Run quality gate checks before release.
+
+Options:
+  --dry-run       Show checks without running them
+  --skip-checks   Skip all checks (return success)
+  --work-dir      Directory to run checks in
+
+Exit Codes:
+  0  - All checks passed
+  1  - One or more checks failed
+  4  - Invalid arguments or configuration
+EOF
+                return 0
+                ;;
+            -*)
+                _qg_log_error "Unknown option: $1"
+                return 4
+                ;;
+            *)
+                tool_name="$1"
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    if [[ -z "$tool_name" ]]; then
+        _qg_log_error "Tool name required"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    # Handle skip-checks
+    if $skip_checks; then
+        _qg_log_warn "Skipping quality checks (--skip-checks)"
+        jq -nc '{
+            tool: "'"$tool_name"'",
+            skipped: true,
+            checks: [],
+            passed: 0,
+            failed: 0,
+            total: 0,
+            duration_ms: 0
+        }'
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Get checks for tool
+    local checks
+    checks=$(qg_get_checks "$tool_name")
+
+    local check_count
+    check_count=$(echo "$checks" | jq 'length')
+
+    if [[ "$check_count" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        _qg_log_info "No quality checks configured for $tool_name"
+        jq -nc --arg tool "$tool_name" '{
+            tool: $tool,
+            skipped: false,
+            checks: [],
+            passed: 0,
+            failed: 0,
+            total: 0,
+            duration_ms: 0
+        }'
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    _qg_log_info "Running $check_count quality check(s) for $tool_name"
+    $dry_run && _qg_log_info "(dry-run mode)"
+    echo ""
+
+    # Run each check
+    local results=()
+    local total_start_ms total_end_ms
+    local passed=0 failed=0
+
+    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
+        total_start_ms=$(python3 -c 'import time; print(int(time.time() * 1000))')
+    else
+        total_start_ms=$(($(date +%s) * 1000))
+    fi
+
+    while IFS= read -r cmd; do
+        [[ -z "$cmd" ]] && continue
+
+        local result
+        result=$(_qg_run_single_check "$cmd" "$work_dir" "$dry_run")
+        results+=("$result")
+
+        local check_passed
+        check_passed=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.passed')
+        if [[ "$check_passed" == "true" ]]; then
+            ((passed++))
+        else
+            ((failed++))
+        fi
+    done < <(echo "$checks" | jq -r '.[]')
+
+    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
+        total_end_ms=$(python3 -c 'import time; print(int(time.time() * 1000))')
+    else
+        total_end_ms=$(($(date +%s) * 1000))
+    fi
+
+    local total_duration_ms=$((total_end_ms - total_start_ms))
+
+    echo ""
+
+    # Build results JSON
+    local checks_json
+    if [[ ${#results[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        checks_json=$(printf '%s\n' "${results[@]}" | jq -s '.')
+    else
+        checks_json='[]'
+    fi
+
+    local result_json
+    result_json=$(jq -nc \
+        --arg tool "$tool_name" \
+        --argjson dry_run "$dry_run" \
+        --argjson checks "$checks_json" \
+        --argjson passed "$passed" \
+        --argjson failed "$failed" \
+        --argjson total "$check_count" \
+        --argjson duration_ms "$total_duration_ms" \
+        '{
+            tool: $tool,
+            dry_run: $dry_run,
+            skipped: false,
+            checks: $checks,
+            passed: $passed,
+            failed: $failed,
+            total: $total,
+            duration_ms: $duration_ms
+        }')
+
+    # Output result
+    echo "$result_json"
+
+    # Log summary
+    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
+        _qg_log_error "Quality gates FAILED: $passed/$check_count passed"
+        return 1
+    else
+        _qg_log_ok "Quality gates passed: $passed/$check_count checks (${total_duration_ms}ms)"
+        return 0
+    fi
+}
+
+# Export functions
+export -f qg_get_checks qg_run_checks _qg_run_single_check
